documents.source_expression: ✨Save for later The truth is, companies don’t always reward those who work the hardest—they reward those who are the most visible. It’s not just about keeping your head down and completing tasks. To advance in your career, you need to be seen, heard, and recognized. Here’s how to play the game: 1 Meet Decision Makers: Make a conscious effort to network with influential people in your organization. These are the individuals who have the power to influence your career growth. Attend meetings, participate in discussions, and make your presence known. 2 Engage in High-Profile Projects: Volunteer for projects that are visible to senior leaders and that align with the company’s strategic goals. This will not only showcase your skills but also position you as someone who contributes to the bigger picture. 3 Understand What Matters to Leadership: Pay attention to what senior leaders prioritize. This could be innovation, cost-cutting, or expanding into new markets. Align your efforts with these priorities to demonstrate that you are in tune with the company’s direction and goals. 4 Promote Your Achievements: Don’t shy away from sharing your successes. Whether through regular updates to your manager or sharing insights in team meetings, make sure your contributions are known. 5 Be Strategic: Think about your actions and how they align with your career goals. Build a personal brand that highlights your strengths and demonstrates your value to the organization. Remember, it’s all a game, and the best players know who to connect with and how to make their efforts count. It’s about balancing hard work with strategic visibility to ensure your efforts lead to recognition and advancement. #925mastery #careergrowth #careerprogression #careermotivation #careertips #officememes #officememe #careerpath #jobtips #jobinterviews #officejokes #officejob #careermemes #memesdaily #officehumour #officehumor #officefun #9to5memes #workmemes #careerdevelopment #careercoaching #careeradvice #dailymemes #memes #workplacememes #salarymemes #workplacehumor #jobinterview #interview

documents.source_expression: 😱 Recruiters only scan resumes for 4 seconds. If your resume is hard to read, or if the template is messy, they are going to delete it and move on to the 100 other resumes that they must go through. Here are 2 Tips on how to make your resume look better to recruiters on that initial scan 1️⃣ Most importantly chose the right template for your level of experience. If you have more than 10 years of experience you can have 2 pages, less than 10 years you just need a one-page resume. 🎯Here is the correct resume format: Name Contact Information Your Title or Titles of the Jobs You Are Applying For Executive Summary Professional Experience Skills Education. That’s it. ✅Your template needs to be clean and easy to read. Make sure the headings on your resume are all the same size, format it and pick a professional font. 2️⃣ Do NOT use the word I on your resume. 🔥Bonus tip- Your current job should be written in present tense and your past jobs in past tense. Go to TheInterviewology.com for FREE resume resources. We have 100 Action Words to use on your resume, 2 free resume template, and a Guide on how to write a killer resume #resumetips #resume #jobinterview #jobsearchtips #applytoajob #howtogetajob

documents.source_expression: Here’s the deal⬇️ As visually appealing as these Canva Resume Templates are, they are not Applicant Tracking System (ATS) Friendly. Here’s what you need instead: Format➡️Simple and boring is best. Name and contact info with LinkedIn url. Headline if you’re experienced and have an Objective over a Summary. Personally, your work history is your summary, and having an objective highlights your past accomplishments and what it is that you’re looking for. Content➡️Your Work History needs to be in chronological order and use strong keywords and action verbs. Don’t repeat the same words like “collaborated” or “managed”. Use other words like “spearheaded”, “led”, “created and implemented”, etc. Length➡️1-2 pages. If you have less than 10 years of work experience, try and keep to 1 page, but it is perfectly acceptable to go into 2 pages. Just know, anything over 2 pages is not going to be focused on. Recruiters spend a few seconds reviewing resumes, and they spend the most time on the last 2 positions. Finally, no “references available upon request” is necessary. Most employers are not able to actually do a reference check, they just do an employment verification. There are of course exceptions to this depending on the industry, but most large organizations don’t require a reference check. Don’t have this on your resume, and instead have a separate document with your references. And make sure to convert it to a PDF prior to applying or sending your resume out to connections. 💻 If you are active in your job search and need an ATS friendly resume, comment “resume” and I’ll send you the template that I’ve used for years to help thousands of candidates and clients receive interviews. [Disclaimer- this is a paid template, but for LESS than a cup of ☕️]. Reach out if you have any other q’s. I’m rooting for you! #recruiter #resumetips #jobsearch #jobapplications #corporate #careercoach

documents.source_expression: How to Create a Successful Mastermind ↓↓ Step 1 : Set a clear intention of what the purpose of the group will be for. Will be focused on business, health, relationships, mindset? Get clear so you can attract the right people. Step 2. Decide what kind of people you want in the group. Think about the kind of people who have strengths in your areas of weaknesses and vice versa. This is how can build a diverse group that could mutually benefit each other. Step 3: Decide on a time-frame. Some high-ticket masterminds can range from a 2-day experience to even a year-long membership. Decide what you’d what would align for you and the people you want to bring together. Step 4 : Set a price. Although some masterminds are free, I’d suggest charging for it. People who pay, pay attention and you want to attract people who are truly invested in the experience. Have fun networking ladies! 👑 . . . #networkingtips #networking #levelup #personaldevelopment #highvaluewomen #thinkandgrowrich

documents.source_expression: You should be able to answer these 5 standard interview questions ahead of your next job interview but if you can’t, bookmark this video and record yourself answering these questions. The fastest way to get better at interviewing is to practice. 5 Standard Interview Questions everyone should know the answer to 1- Tell me about yourself. *A great formula to follow is past, present and future. Tell them what you went to college for, why you do what you do, what you are doing in your current job that qualifies you for this position and future is that you see yourself in this organization where you are interviewing. 2- What are your strengths and weaknesses? *Strengths are easy and obvious, but weaknesses can be harder to come up with. Remember a good rule of thumb is to share a weakness that is true for you and can be construed into a strength. Here are some great weaknesses to give, “I have a hard time delegating” “I am impatient” “I am very detail oriented and can lose sight of the big picture” “I have a hard time asking for help” 3- Why do you want to work here? *Do not tell them all the things you like about the company or all the things the company can do for them. Tell them how you can help the company achieve it’s goals. 4- Where do you see yourself in 5 years? The only right answer is “I see myself at this organization.” Do not tell them you want to get married and have kids or go to grad school. 5- Why should we hire you? *Do not reiterate your strengths. Instead think about what makes you unique. What makes you different than the competition because the question is, why should we hire YOU. They want to know what makes you YOU. For more interview questions to practice and tips on how to make a great impression in a job interview, get an Interviewology Profile #howtoanswerinterviewquestions #jobinterview #career #job #howtogetanewjob #howtofindajob #Corporatejobs #interviewpreparation #careeradvice

documents.source_expression: ✨️Be a Connector, Not Just a Collector: Networking isn't a numbers game. Focus on quality connections and fostering relationships. When you meet someone interesting, think about who else in your network they might benefit from knowing, and make thoughtful introductions. ✨️Follow Up better : A simple "it was nice meeting you" email is forgettable. Craft a personalized follow-up email within 24 hours. Reference something specific you discussed and offer a way to stay connected – share a relevant article, connect on LinkedIn, or suggest grabbing coffee to learn more about their work ✨️ Active Listening: Networking isn't a monologue about yourself. Ask insightful questions, truly listen to their responses, and find common ground. People remember those who make them feel heard and valued. ✨️Embrace the Power of Virtual Connections: In today's digital world, your network isn't limited to in-person events. Join online communities relevant to your field, participate in industry Twitter chats, or connect with professionals on LinkedIn. Virtual interactions can lead to real-world opportunities. ✨️Offer Help Before You Ask: People are more receptive to those who offer value first. Identify ways you can be a resource to someone in your network – proofread their resume, connect them with someone in your contacts, or offer feedback on a project. Genuine help builds trust and strengthens relationships. Comment down " network " and I'll send over some free resources to help you with your career journey 🎀 #womenincorporate #networkingtips #girlssupportgirls #womenempowerment #internships

documents.source_expression: @advicewitherin 🤯 One of my best pieces of job interview advice is to think about every question backwards. Don’t think “hmm, what is my answer to this question…” Instead think “what answer do they want to hear and how do I fit into that?” “Tell me about yourself?” ❌ Don’t tell them about yourself. ✅ Tell them about you, their next favorite hire. “Why do you want to work here?” ❌ Don’t talk about how you heard it pays well and will look good on your resume ✅ Tell them about how you want to contribute value to a company you believe in. “Tell me about a time when…?” ❌ don’t ramble about some half baked memory you have. ✅ tell them about your character and how you behave in difficult situations Always answer the question behind the question 🧐 keep it positive, keep it forward, keep it you. There’s only one you! ☀️ You got this! 👏🏼 #jobsearch #jobinterview

documents.source_expression: 1. Reading the room In a workplace context this basically means recognising and responding to the collective mood; paying attention to body language, tone of voice, facial expressions and emotional responses to gauge how people are feeling and whether a conversation is going well. Examples: knowing when to speak and when to stay quiet, when to push back and when to let something go, knowing when the energy in a meeting has shifted and you need to adapt. 2. Managing up Proactively building a strong working relationship with your manager rather than just responding to what they ask of you. Anticipating what your manager needs, aligning your work with their priorities and flagging potential problems early WITH solutions. Making your manager’s job easier can help advance your career as a result. 3. Knowing how to communicate bad news Handling difficult conversations is a very under appreciated skill imo. It involves flagging a mistake early, raising a concern with a senior colleague, telling someone something they don’t want to hear. How you handle those moments says a lot about your professionalism. 4. Building genuine relationships Not networking in the performative LinkedIn sense but actually investing in the people around you and being someone people enjoy working with. And as a bonus, when you build genuine relationships, people remember you, vouch for you and open doors for you. 5. Emotional regulation Staying composed under pressure and not letting your emotions make decisions for you. Everyone notices how you handle a difficult situations and losing your composure even once can define how people see you for a long time. 6. Knowing when to ask for help and when to figure it out yourself The skill is knowing the difference: when to have a go, when to flag uncertainty early and how to ask questions in a way that shows you’ve already thought about it. One of the biggest mistakes you can make in corporate is not asking for help when you should have. #corporatelife #corporategirlie #corporatetips #careeradvice #corporateskills #careerskills

documents.source_expression: Every job seeker knows this feeling 😓⁠ ⁠ You find the perfect entry-level job posting, get excited, and start applying, only to spiral wondering how many other candidates are going for the same role. ⁠ ⁠ NACE's 2026 Job Outlook found employers described the job market for this year's grads as "fair," in a tight labor market that's been holding steady. ⁠ ⁠ The good news is hiring picked up as the year went on, with employers projecting a stronger increase in Class of 2026 hires by spring. On top of that, 70% of employers now use skills-based hiring, meaning your experience and demonstrated abilities can matter more than the applicant count you're staring at. ⁠ ⁠ Don't let the numbers talk you out of applying. Follow for more on the entry-level job search, internships, and navigating the 2026 job market.⁠ ⁠ #jobseekers #wayup #2026NID

documents.source_expression: The job market feels broken. AI is screening resumes (and humans, sometimes). Entry-level jobs want five years of experience. And everyone’s telling you to “just network.” So… how do you actually start? This week, Pulitzer Prize-winning investigative journalist @jodikantor joins Laura and Kristina to talk about building a career when the path isn’t clear, why struggle is part of the process (not proof you’re failing), and how to find your way in an intimidating job market. Also in this week’s episode: 🔥Why “struggling well” is one of the most important career skills you can develop 🔥How getting fired from her college newspaper almost ended Jodi’s journalism career before it even began 🔥What actually makes a great boss 🔥The “back doors” that still exist even when the digital front door feels impossible to get through If you’re early in your career, feeling stuck, or literally wondering how to start… start here. Thanks to @jodikantor for joining us. Watch the full episode on YouTube or listen wherever you get your podcasts. #AllTheCoolGirlsGetFired #JodiKantor #CareerAdvice #EarlyCareer #Podcast

documents.source_expression: During an influential national student summit held in West Palm Beach, United States, Charlie Kirk delivered a highly motivating keynote speech addressing the critical life choices that shape young lives and define their future success in a rapidly changing world. This powerful address focused heavily on how the rising generation of Americans can successfully navigate the complicated transition into the competitive professional world by deliberately rejecting mainstream cultural clichés that often lead to professional mediocrity and personal confusion. Sharing these practical, real-world insights helps inspire young conservative advocates to approach their academic endeavors and future career paths with a renewed sense of personal responsibility, intellectual clarity, and unwavering direction. The core of his message directly challenged the popular modern advice of simply following your emotions rather than focusing on your practical, objective strengths and actual abilities. He explained that individuals should systematically identify what they are naturally good at by actively listening to the genuine compliments and positive feedback they consistently receive from the people around them. By encouraging people to aggressively pursue their unique, God-given talents instead of pursuing fleeting, unrealistic passions, this straightforward logic provides a much more stable, practical, and rewarding foundation for long-term professional success.#TraditionalValues #CharlieKirk #TurningPointUSA #CareerAdvice #PersonalGrowth

documents.source_expression: Tell me about yourself interview answer 👇 When an interviewer asks “Tell me about yourself”, it isn’t an introduction, it’s an elimination round. They’re asking one thing: “Why should we hire you?” Most candidates here talk about: ❌ Their age ❌ Where they’re from ❌ Their hobbies But the candidates who get hired talk about: ✅ What they do ✅ What they’ve achieved ✅ The value they’ll bring to the role That’s what recruiters remember. If you’re preparing for your next job interview, don’t memorize answers; build a story that connects your skills, achievements, and the role you’re applying for. Need help with your interview preparation? Careerflow’s AI Interview Copilot helps you generate role-specific interview answers, practice common interview questions, and receive personalized feedback before your interview. 💬 Comment “INTERVIEW” and we’ll send you the tool. 📌 Save this for your next interview. documents.source_expression: Stop applying into the void. Here's why it keeps happening & how to fix it. 👇⁠ ⁠ Most job sites weren't built for you. They were built for people with 5+ years of experience who already know someone on the inside. When a student or recent grad applies there, the resume goes in, and nothing comes out.⁠ ⁠ That's not a you problem. That's a platform problem.⁠ ⁠ WayUp is different. It's built specifically for college students and recent grads. Which means the employers on it are actually looking for people at your level. No "entry-level requires 3 years of experience." No black hole. Just real opportunities with companies that want to hire early-career talent.⁠ ⁠ Here's what you get:⁠ ⁠ → Exclusive jobs and internships you won't find anywhere else⁠ → Direct connections to employers who are actively recruiting students⁠ → Career tools to help you stand out before you even hit apply⁠ ⁠ If you've been doing everything right and still hearing nothing back, the problem isn't your resume. It's where you're sending it.⁠ ⁠ ⁠ #CollegeStudents #Internships #CareerTips

documents.source_expression: Comment “PROMPT” and I’ll send you the three prompts I had used. No, it wasn’t luck. I changed the way I wrote my resume using these 3 AI prompts before uploading it. 👇 Save these prompts: 1️⃣ ATS Resume Optimizer “Act as an ATS recruiter. Analyze my resume for a [Job Role] and rewrite it to score above 90% ATS. Improve keywords, formatting, and bullet points without adding fake experience.” 2️⃣ Recruiter Eye Test “Review my resume like a hiring manager. Tell me the top 10 reasons you would reject it in the first 10 seconds, then rewrite those sections to make them stronger.” 3️⃣ Achievement Booster “Rewrite every bullet point in my resume using strong action verbs and measurable impact. Add quantifiable results wherever possible without inventing information.” 💡 Most people don’t get rejected because they lack skills. They get rejected because their resume never gets noticed. Save this post, try these prompts, and upload your updated resume before your next application. #ResumeTips #ATSResume #ResumeBuilder #JobSearch #FreshersJobs (InterviewTips CareerGrowth AIForJobs ChatGPTPrompts ResumeWriting LinkedInTips Hiring Placement JobHunt CareerAdvice)

documents.source_expression: Most career & alumni centers are sitting on a very compelling success story. They just sometimes can't tell, but it's NOT their fault. Employer satisfaction. Starting salaries by major. Time to first job. Placement rates by industry. This is the data that justifies your budget, satisfies accreditation requirements, and convinces prospective students to choose your program. This data matters. The problem isn't that the data doesn't exist; it's that collecting it manually is exhausting or impossible. The result is a report built on incomplete information that undersells everything your team actually accomplished... That has to stop. 12twenty's Outcome Data Analytics Hub automates outcome data collection, integrates with your existing systems, and generates accreditation-ready reports in a matter of clicks instead of days. documents.source_expression: The most common interview mistake isn't a bad answer. It's a good answer, badly delivered. You know the STAR method. You've got the right experience. But somewhere between thinking it and saying it, it turns into a ramble, and the interviewer checks out halfway through. That's not a knowledge problem. That's a delivery problem, and it's fixable. Here's how: ✅ Go to Careerflow.ai and sign up ✅ Open the Mock Interview tool ✅ Pick your scenario and answer real interview questions The AI listens to your actual answers, breaks down your clarity, structure, and confidence, and tells you exactly what's not landing, before a recruiter finds out first. documents.source_expression: ⬇️ Here are the 5 prompts to revamp your Resume - 1️⃣ To Review your Resume ✅ Act like a Professional Resume Expert. I am a (your position) who wants to get a job in (target role/ industry). My target companies are (target companies). Please help me review my resume by making sure that the format + structure is correct and there are no grammatical errors. Upload your resume as a PDF / Word File. 2️⃣ To Optimize Professional Summary ✅ Act like a resume optimization expert. I’m applying for a (job title) role in the (industry name) sector. Can you improve the professional summary section of my resume to highlight my key strengths and achievements? Here’s my current summary: (Insert your summary here) 3️⃣ To find Keywords for a Role ✅ Act like a recruiter/ an ATS specialist. I’m applying for a (job title) position at (company name). I will be sharing the job description here, help me pick out keywords from the same that I can include in my resume. Please also suggest generalised keywords that I must include. My goal is to make sure that my resume passes the ATS software and is shortlisted by the hiring managers. Here’s the job description: (Insert JD here) 4️⃣ To Make your Work Experince Bullet Points More Impactful ✅ Act like a Career Coach/ recruiter. I’m a (your position) with (X years) of experience. Please rewrite the following responsibility as a result-oriented bullet point, focusing on achievements and quantifiable impact. Here are my responsibilities: (Insert responsibility here). 5️⃣ To Find Relevant Skills ✅ Act like an HR Manager. I’m a (your position) looking to apply for a (job title) role in (industry). Please suggest the most in-demand skills I should highlight in my resume for this position. My goal is to stand out to hiring managers. Comment ‘Prompts’ in the caption to receive the prompts in your DM!👇💬 Follow @riddhiii_pobara for more such tips! ✅ Share this with someone to help them in their job search! ✨ #resume #resumetips #resumerevamp #resumereview #chatgpt #chatgptprompts #writingprompts #chatgpttips #jobsearch #jobsearchstrategies #jobseekers #career #careerdevelopment #careeradvice

documents.source_expression: July 1 is one of the wildest times in a resident’s life. It’s where you’ve finally arrived at the moment you’ve worked so hard to get to. Yet, there’s some trepidation associated with not wanting to mess up, look silly or cause harm. It’s the transition as a newly minted chief resident where you are leading, where your actions are so important because of the lasting impressions they have on residents and medical students below you and the attendings guiding you. It’s the reset of an academic cycle for attendings that is filled with excitement - reminding you of your why as you witness and facilitate all of these transitions and growth right before your eyes. Not to be cliché, but it’s true; the hours are truly long but the days are short. Soak it all in, never stop learning and don’t stop teaching, no matter where you are in the process. #academicmedicine #thevoiceboxdoctor #medstudentlife #residentlife #internlife

documents.source_expression: This Boolean search hack finds the latest job posts in seconds. Most people don’t even know it exists. documents.source_expression: #StevesPeeves - ONLY using a resume. We are kicking off @EagleUniversity this week where we will have high school and college students from all over North America learning how to get a 7-year head start on their career. One of the principles they will be learning is about a “brag book.” 10 times more powerful than a resume, a brag book and all of its veriations has the power to differentiate you from everyone else. EU team leader @KateeHull explains what a brag book is and what it does. If you want something different than what everyone else gets, you have to be willing to do things others don’t do. A brag book is just one of the many “different” things students at Eagle U learn to differentiate themselves from others. But using ONLY a resume…? That’s what everyone else does! #StevesPeeves

documents.source_expression: Not every ADHD brain thrives in the same environment. In this video, I talk about two careers that might surprise you: 👥 Social work & community support 👨🏾‍🍳 Culinary arts Many people with ADHD are deeply empathetic and enjoy meaningful, people-focused work. Others thrive in fast-paced, high-pressure environments where every day is different. That doesn’t mean these careers are right for everyone with ADHD. For some people, they could be the exact opposite. The goal isn’t to find the perfect ADHD job—it’s to find an environment that works with your brain rather than against it. As an occupational therapist, that’s what I’m always thinking about: the relationship between the person, the environment, and the occupation. 💬 Which career has suited your brain best? Or which one definitely wasn’t for you? 📌 Save this for later #ADHD #Neurodiversity #CareerAdvice #OccupationalTherapy #ADHDAwareness

documents.source_expression: How to automate your job search with ChatGPT? No more ten tabs open before 9am just to feel behind for the rest of the day. Here’s the thing about job hunting: Most of it isn’t skill, it’s repetition. Same job boards, same scroll, same feeling of “did I miss something better yesterday.” That’s exactly the kind of work AI job search tools were built to take off your plate. Instead of manually searching every day, set ChatGPT to do it for you: ✅ Open ChatGPT → Settings → Notifications → Tasks, and turn on push and email alerts. ✅Then paste in one specific ChatGPT job search prompt. (This part matters more than people think, a vague prompt gets you vague, useless results) That’s the whole setup. Every morning from here on, you’ll wake up to a curated list of relevant jobs, with direct links, already waiting for you. This is one of the easiest ways to automate your job search right now, and most people don’t even know ChatGPT’s Tasks feature exists yet. Want the exact prompt? Comment “PROMPT” and I’ll DM it to you! Follow @careerflow.ai, we share hacks like this often. #chatgpt #chatgptprompts #jobsearch #careeradvice #careerflowai

documents.source_expression: For years, most of us believed career growth followed one formula. ➡️ Gain experience. ➡️ Become a manager. ➡️ Move away from execution. ➡️ Focus on strategy. But the workplace is changing. Companies are no longer looking only at how many years you’ve worked. They’re asking a different question: Can you create business value today? The professionals growing fastest aren’t necessarily the ones with the longest résumés. They’re the ones who: * Keep learning. * Stay close to execution. * Understand the business. * Connect their work to measurable outcomes. Your experience is still valuable. But experience that stops evolving eventually stops differentiating you. Have you seen experienced professionals reporting to younger managers in your company? I’d love to hear your perspective. 👇 Let’s discuss #CareerGrowth #CorporateLife #Leadership #Promotion #CareerAdvice Workplace Manager ProfessionalDevelopment FutureOfWork LeadershipDevelopment CorporateReality BusinessLeadership WorkingProfessionals Careers DeepaliGola

documents.source_expression: The best place to find brand deals isn’t Instagram! ⬇️ I’ve got a secret weapon 🤫 to unlock more brand partnership opportunities. This is one of my best kept “secret” strategies and let me tell ya- it works like a charm! Instead of Instagram? Go to @linkedin !!✨ ⭐️ Here’s how to leverage this powerful platform: 👉🏼 Search for “Influencer Strategist” on LinkedIn. These are the independent contractors hired to connect brands with creators like you. 👉🏼 Connect with them and engage in conversations they’re having. Usually they’re talking to other influencer strategists! Connect with these others as well and begin building relationships. Join in on their conversations and lead with value! Give your 2 cents to the convo! 👉🏼 Network, Network, Network: By building strong connections with these industry professionals, you’ll gain access to a much wider pool of brands and opportunities. 👱🏼‍♀️ Remember, it’s not just about the brands; it’s about the people behind them. Because there’s really no such thing as a “brand” deal…The big “brand” isn’t making a deal. People are making the deals. 🤝 Build relationships with people in the industry and you will get more “brand” deals!! 🫶🏻 Trust me: Building ONE relationship with an influencer strategist is like building TEN relationships with brands. 🚀 Ready to take your influencer career to the next level? Give this LinkedIn strategy a try and let me know how it goes!! 😍 💬 Drop a comment below if you have any questions or want to share your experience using this strategy!! ⬇️ #influencermarketing #branddeals #socialmediatips #contentcreatortips #influencertips #linkedintips #creatoreconomy #influencercommunity #creatorcommunity #creatortips #brandpartnerships #linkedin #buildingcommunity #connectingcommunities #microinfluencers #workwithbrands #networkingtips

documents.source_expression: 3️⃣ Impressive Questions to Ask During any Job Interview 3️⃣ Why is the position open? If it’s new, ask them why. Did they get new clients or a new project? Or perhaps the incumbent got promoted, then ask them what that person did so well. 2️⃣ Aside from technical skills and the job description, what are some soft skills that would make someone successful in this position? 1️⃣ What are some projects that you’re currently working on? 📌PRO TIP, Don’t wait till the end of the interview to ask questions. Ask questions throughout the interview. It will feel more conversational and you will look prepared and interested. documents.source_expression: Stop wasting your time going to events… if you’re not going to let people know that you’re in the room 🫣 Opportunities don’t find you, people do… so introduce yourself to the room. I’ll be honest, I’ve been guilty of it too. But I’m breaking the habit, and every time I do, it pays off. Too many women attend events, take notes, and leave the same way they came—invisible. I’ve seen it time and time again. Meanwhile, the men? No hesitation. No shrinking. No missed opportunities. Showing up isn’t enough. You need to be remembered. 3 Ways i Make My Presence Known at Any Event: 👀 Be intentional – Scan the agenda, know who you want to meet, and set a goal to make that connection. 🙋🏽‍♀️ Leverage Q&As – When the mic is open, take it. A simple “Can you expand on that?” or “I’m curious to know…” will do the job, don’t overthink it. 🎤Introduce yourself properly – Stand up, say your full name, what you do, THEN ask your question. Too many people waste this moment! Events are free opportunities to pitch yourself. Investors, customers, collaborators—they’re all there. But they won’t find you if they don’t know your name. So next time? Don’t just be in the room. OWN the room! #makeyourpresenceknown #networkingtips #womeninbusiness #entrepreneurship #personalbranding #treasuretress

documents.source_expression: work tip Wednesday: a simple yet stand out way to build relationships every Wednesday I get ready and talk about career, leadership and a piece of advice that I have learned working for some of the worlds biggest brands **Send a handwritten note.** After a conversation with a mentor or senior leader, a thoughtful note communicates professionalism, gratitude, and intentionality. It’s a small gesture that leaves a lasting impression—and sets you apart in a digital-first world. Here are 3 ways to make it easy and consistent: 1. Keep a Stationery Kit in Your Office Have note cards, envelopes, and stamps ready to go. When everything’s within reach, it becomes easy 2. Do it immediately As soon as you’ve had the meeting, or even if it is top of mind - write the note. 3. Use a Simple but Consistent Framework but tailor some lines to make it yours Start with a framework: Thank them for their time Mention a specific takeaway Close with appreciation or a next step This keeps your message personal without starting from scratch each time. A handwritten note doesn’t take much — I get handwritten notes so infrequently I think it’s really special, so save nearly every single one!! GRWM products skincare - glass skin peptide serum by @skinceuticals and @rhode moisturizer + spf tinted moisturizer “just enough” by @jonesroadbeauty I wear medium honey and blush @westmanatelier lipstick and highlighter brow soap by @inglot_cosmetics blowout by @dyson and my Amazon rollers!! tutorial in past Reel #networking #networkingtips #careeradvice #behindthescenes

documents.source_expression: The interview mistake most candidates make at the very end! 🚩 You've answered all their questions brilliantly. The interview is wrapping up. But if you walk out without explicitly stating why you want THIS job and why you're the perfect fit, you've missed a crucial opportunity ❌ Many candidates assume: • Their enthusiasm is obvious • Their qualifications speak for themselves • The interviewer already knows why they're a match But leaving these things unsaid is a huge risk! The final moments of an interview create a lasting impression. Before leaving, make sure to clearly articulate: • Why this specific role and company excite you • How your experience directly addresses their needs • Your enthusiasm for the opportunity to contribute This simple strategy ensures you leave the interviewer with clarity about your interest and fit - exactly what they need to advocate for you in hiring discussions 🌟 Comment "ELITE" below for more interview techniques that get results! 💼 #executiverecruiter #eliterecruiter #jobmarket2025 #profoliosai #resume #jobstrategy #interviewtips #jobsearch #interviewtechniques #careeradvice #jobinterviews #hiringprocess #interviewprep #interviewskills #jobhunting #interviewquestions #careergrowth #jobseekers #interviewsuccess #professionaladvice

documents.source_expression: How to prep for an interview in under 1 hour ✅ Send yourself this post so you don’t lose it. 1️⃣ (10 min) Do a gap analysis: Copy the job description from the advert and paste into a google doc. Form there highlights the bullet points you have (blue) vs don’t have (red) from the job description. Under each one of the ones you have, add examples when you showed those skills (ex: managed an event). For the ones you don’t have, add anything that could complement that skill. 2️⃣ (10 min) Research the company: Don’t just skim the website - dig deeper. Use ChatGPT or Perplexity with a prompt like: “I’m interviewing for [Company] for a [Role]. Tell me everything I need to know for the interview. What are the top priorities and challenges for [Company] in 2025?” Look for product launches, leadership updates, recent news, and how they make money. Show you understand their world. 3️⃣(10 min) Find the likely questions: Head to Glassdoor and look for real interview questions from that company. Then put together in a doc the 10 most common behavioral questions to start preparing for them (like: Tell me about a time you failed). Pro tip: Don’t memorize - just prepare one story you can flex across a few questions. 4️⃣ (20 min) Practice your answers: For behavioral interview questions use STARL: Situation, Task, Action, Result - and what you Learned. To prepare, use tools like Google Interview Warmup or ChatGPT to test your answers. Prompt idea: “Give me feedback on this answer using the STARL method. Here’s the question and here’s my answer…” 5️⃣ (10 min) Prep 3–5 smart questions to ask them: Asking food questions is key. Aim for: 2 role-specific (show you understand the day-to-day). 2 company-specific (prove you did your research). This is your chance to show you’re not just job hunting - you’re intentional. You got this 💪 🔥If you want a cheat sheet to prepare for interviews, just comment INTERVIEW below 😉 #jobapplication #jobsearch #jobhunt #jobopportunity #interviewtips #interviewquestions #interviewprep #interview #bigtech #jobseeker

documents.source_expression: I’ve gotten a lot of questions on what my internship looks like and how to do well in the corporate intern setting, so here are 5 things I’ve found to that helped me be successful in my first week as an intern: 1. Show Up Early and Ready to Learn Arrive a few minutes early to meetings with a notepad and pen in hand. Being prepared shows that you’re professional, engaged, and eager to make the most of your time there. Take notes, ask questions, and be a sponge for everything happening around you. 2. Have a Good and Humble Attitude As simple as it is, approach your internship with a servant-hearted mindset. Be positive, teachable, and respectful. Your attitude will often matter more than your skillset, especially early on. 3. Work Quickly and Take Initiative Finish your tasks promptly and with excellence, and if you have a question, ask. If you have free time, don’t sit on your phone, look for ways to contribute. Ask your manager or teammates how you can help or if there’s anything else you can jump in on. Proactivity stands out. 4. Don’t Be Shy. Start Conversations. Take the initiative to talk to your manager, team members, or anyone you interact with. Most people want to share their story and are excited to help you. Building relationships early opens doors and turns your internship into a network-building opportunity. 5. Move Your Body Outside of Work: Sitting at a desk all day can take a serious toll on your physical and mental well-being. Make it a priority to move, whether it’s lifting weights, going for a run, or stretching after work. Keeping your body active will give you more energy and improve focus . . . . . . #finance #financeintern #internships #intern #internlife #advice #businessadvice

documents.source_expression: In 10+ years of recruiting, I can tell you this👇 Recruiters don’t have time to fully read resumes. Weird I know…. Considering that’s kinda their job 😂 But really what they do is scan for immediate relevance. This means the first thing they’ll review is your Headline (Job title) and your professional summary — those top 3–5 lines right under your name and headline. 💬 If this section doesn’t grab attention in 5 seconds, the rest of your resume won’t get read. Here’s how to fix it 👇 🧠 Think of your summary like your shop window: You’re not just listing who you are. You’re positioning yourself for the job you want next. So instead of saying: ❌ “Experienced marketing professional seeking new opportunities to grow…” Say: ✅ “Marketing Manager | 8+ years scaling B2B brands | Drove $2M revenue growth via paid + organic channels” Now you’re giving them: ✔️ Your current title ✔️ Years of experience ✔️ 2–3 keywords from the job description ✔️ A high-impact result That’s what stops the scroll. That’s what gets interviews. 🛠 Bonus: Use this ChatGPT prompt to craft yours: “Based on this job spec [paste job ad], and this experience [paste your experience], write a 3-line professional summary that includes my job title, experience, keywords, and one measurable result.” If you want a free resume template with space for this exact structure it’s included inside my online community The Job Seekers Circle. 📌Comment COMMUNITY and I’ll send you the link to get access #resumetips #careercoach #jobsearchstrategy #cvwriting #getnoticed #linkedinresume #personalbranding #jobseekers #recruitingtips

documents.source_expression: If I could go back in time, I’d tell my 20-year-old self these powerful networking lessons - the same ones that helped me build meaningful relationships, unlock career opportunities, and grow faster than I ever expected. Whether you’re an introvert, student, aspiring entrepreneur - this is for YOU. Follow @ananyamentors for more such career advice ❤️ 🔟 Be Curious, Not Just Polite Ask thoughtful questions. People remember how you made them feel heard, not just small talk. Be genuinely curious about others. 9️⃣ Don’t Just Collect Business Cards - Build Real Connections Quality beats quantity. One solid connection is worth more than 10 cold intros. 8️⃣ Your Online Presence Is Your First Impression Your LinkedIn is your digital handshake. Keep it sharp, active, and aligned with your goals. 7️⃣ Offer Value Before You Ask for Favors Networking isn’t about taking - it’s about exchanging. Share resources, amplify their work, or offer help with no expectations. 6️⃣ Say Yes to Events, Even If You’re Nervous The best connections often come from spontaneous interactions. Growth lives just outside your comfort zone. 5️⃣ Follow Up Like a Pro (But Don’t Be Pushy) Send a short, specific follow-up within 24–48 hours. Personalize it. Show appreciation. Most people don’t follow up - you’ll stand out if you do. 4️⃣ Be a Connector, Not Just a Climber Introduce people who should know each other. When you create value between others, you become valuable too. 3️⃣ Build Long-Term, Not Transactional Relationships Think 5-year relationships, not 5-minute favors. Consistency, kindness, and staying in touch build trust. 2️⃣ Network Across, Not Just Up Your peers today are tomorrow’s leaders. Don’t just chase CEOs - grow with the people beside you. 1️⃣ Play the Long Game - You Never Know Who Becomes Who That intern might be a future founder. That quiet person in the corner? Your next business partner. Treat everyone with respect. Follow @ananyamentors for more such advice. Save this post & share it with a friend ❤️ [networking, networking tips, introvert tips, career advice, overcome shyness, how to network] #networkingtips #careercoach #networking #networkingadvice

documents.source_expression: 1. Great work no longer speaks for itself. It still matters, but there’s an additional step: Showcase your work constantly—pull metrics, highlight the impact, send updates to stakeholders. Visibility is now part of the job. 2. Introverts have it harder. Small talk before meetings, cross-department projects, casual networking—these matter more than most people admit. Embrace it, even if it drains you. 3. Companies exist to make money, not to create jobs. Build a second income stream now. Invest, build a product, understand business (don’t just work in one). You can’t rely on a single job. 4. Build your brand outside the company. Pick a project or skill and start talking about it online. Make yourself known independently of your employer. 5. Quality over quantity when job hunting. Five strong applications per day beats AI-blasting hundreds per week. 6. Your network is just as important as upskilling. The people you know can determine the opportunities you get. Invest in relationships, not just transactions. 7. Results > Responsibilities. Learn to communicate the value you create. Companies care about impact and results. Frame everything that way. 8. Stop waiting to feel ready. Confidence, and all the other feelings you want, come after you start. Apply for roles that scare you, take on projects above your current skill level. 9. Diversify your skillset. If you’re a programmer, learn marketing. If you’re a project manager, learn data analysis. Specialization is for insects. 10. Diversify your identity. I’ve been laid off twice and had no “identity crisis”. That’s because I play music, read, write, and do several things outside of my job. 👨🏻‍💻I’ve been in tech for almost 10 yrs now and have seen it all. Follow for more career advice @findfulfillingwork.official #career #careeradvice

documents.source_expression: Your resume might sound outdated - and that could be why you’re not landing interviews. 👇 The issue isn’t your experience. It’s your word choice. Let’s fix that. Here’s what to ditch and what to say instead: ❌ Team player ✅ Collaborative professional | Cross-functional contributor ❌ Assisted ✅ Partnered with | Supported key initiatives | Drove results alongside ❌ Managed ✅ Led | Directed | Optimized operations ❌ Detail-oriented ✅ Accuracy-driven | Quality-focused ❌ Implemented ✅ Executed | Enhanced | Streamlined ❌ Responsible for ✅ Delivered | Achieved | Owned outcomes for ❌ Experienced in ✅ Proficient at | Specialized in | Expert in ❌ Hardworking ✅ Results-oriented | Performance-driven ❌ Strong communication skills ✅ Facilitated collaboration across teams | Influenced stakeholders | Presented complex ideas clearly ❌ Responsible for leading ✅ Championed | Mobilized | Directed cross-team initiatives ✨ Pro Tip: Modern resumes sell impact, not buzzwords. Podcast: Diary of a CEO @steven Follow @biz.insider for more applicable business tips.

documents.source_expression: Part 1 | Career advice I NEEDED at 21, not 26. 1. Your degree matters less than the skills you build. 2. Learn communication early. It decides your entire career because confidence beats experience. 3. Don’t chase money first. Chase growth, money follows. 4. Stop trying to impress everyone. Impress yourself. 5. Build your network before you need it. 6. Don’t stay in a job that drains your ambition. 7. Start investing, even if it’s tiny. Time is your real wealth. 8. Learn to ask for opportunities. Closed mouths stay stuck. 9. Work on your confidence. It’s more valuable than talent. 10. Learn one high-income skill. It gives you freedom later. 11. Don’t compare your journey with anyone. Everyone peaks at different times. 12. Take care of your health. A tired mind can’t build a big career. 13. Take risks early. Fear grows with age. 14. Stop waiting for the “perfect moment.” It never comes. 15. Focus on the long game. Your 20s are for building, not proving. Comment “Yes” and I’ll send you the career clarity framework I wish I had at 26. Also share this with a friend in their 20s. If you want personal help, I do 1:1 clarity calls. documents.source_expression: For the longest time, I thought I wasn’t getting interviews because I “wasn’t good enough.” I kept refreshing my inbox… kept checking my phone… kept wondering why no one was calling back — even though I knew the work I’d done deserved to be seen. Then one day I learned the truth: My résumé wasn’t even reaching the hiring managers. It was getting filtered out by something called the ATS — an automated system silently deciding my fate before a human ever could. So I started from zero. I removed the fancy designs. Stripped away the elements ATS couldn’t read. And rebuilt my résumé to be simple, clear, keyword-rich, and system-friendly. And everything changed. Suddenly the same résumé that was getting ignored started getting noticed. Emails turned into calls. Calls turned into interviews. One interview turned into the opportunity that completely rewrote my career. The truth is — you don’t always need “more experience.” Sometimes you just need a résumé that actually makes it past the digital gatekeeper. This reel is a small glimpse of that journey — the transformation that helped me land my job, and the exact format I wish someone had shared with me sooner. If you’ve been applying everywhere and hearing nothing… Trust me, it’s not always you. Sometimes it’s the résumé. And I’ve created a full ATS-friendly template (plus a tutorial) so you don’t have to struggle like I did. Follow and Comment “ATS” and I’ll send it your way. — @gautam.designn Keywords: ats resume, job search journey, resume redesign, product designer resume, job hunting tips, uiux career, interview ready resume #atsresume #resumehelp #jobresume #resumetipsdaily #atsfriendly #resumeformat #jobsearch2025 #jobhuntguide #careertipsdaily #resumebuilder #viralreels #designresume #uxresume #productdesignerresume #jobseekerhelp #careerbuilder #interviewprep #uiuxcareer #jobsearchtips #careeradvice #resumetips #jobseekers #ats #uiuxdesign #uxdesign #productdesign #fyp #foryou #explore

documents.source_expression: I wish someone had told me these things earlier. Here’s what I’ve learned: 1. Your degree matters less than the skills you build. What you can actually do is more important than where you went to school. 2. Learn to communicate effectively because it decides your entire career. Confidence beats experience when you can articulate your value. 3. Don’t chase money first. Chase growth instead, and the money will follow. Focus on learning and the opportunities will come. 4. Your network is your net worth. It’s cliche but it’s so true. The relationships you build matter more than you think, and they’ll open doors you didn’t even know existed. 5. It’s okay to not know what you want to do. Most people don’t, and that’s fine. You can figure it out as you go. 6. Ask for what you want. You won’t get it if you don’t ask, and the worst they can say is no. Most people are too afraid to ask. 7. Failure is part of the process. You’re going to mess up, and that’s okay. It’s how you learn and grow/bounce back. 8. Your first job doesn’t define your career. You can always pivot, change directions, and start over. Nothing in this world is permanent. 9. Invest in yourself. Take courses, read books, learn new skills. The best investment you can make is in your own self growth. 10. Don’t compare yourself to others. Everyone’s journey is different, and comparing yourself will only make you feel behind when you’re not. These are the things I wish I knew earlier. What would you add to this list? 👀 #growth #personalgrowth #personalgrowthjourney #relatable #career #advice #tech

documents.source_expression: Real advice you won’t hear from others cause I care about your success not your feelings. 1.Most “passion” is childish delusion, pick a market that pays stupid money and get good at it. 2.Your degree matters less than the skills you build. 3.Being “nice” and “humble” early keeps you poor and replaceable. Confidence + results opens every door. 4.Stop trying to impress everyone. Impress yourself. 5.Build your network before you need it. 6.Don’t stay in a job that drains your ambition. 7.Start investing, even if it’s tiny. Time is your real wealth. 8.Learn to ask for opportunities. Closed mouths stay stuck. 9.Work on your confidence. It’s more valuable than talent. 10.Learn one high-income skill. It gives you freedom later. 11.Don’t compare your journey with anyone. Everyone peaks at different times. 12.Take care of your health. A tired mind can’t build a big career. 13.Take risks early. Fear grows with age. 14.Stop waiting for the “perfect moment.” It never comes. 15.Accept looking selfish, shallow, and money-obsessed in your 20s if you ever want the option to be generous later. What do you think of this advice? Share this with a friend in their 20s. You’ve got this, talk soon 🩶 - Mikey

documents.source_expression: Most employees think visibility = talking more. It’s the opposite. Visibility = making it easy for others to understand your impact. 👇 1. The “2-line update” After anything important (meeting, task, decision), send this: Line 1: What changed / what we decided Line 2: What I’m doing next + when you’ll see it That’s it. You just became the person who reduces confusion. 2. The “No surprises” rule If something might slip… say it early. Managers don’t hate bad news. They hate discovering it late. 3. Ask for “direction,” not “feedback” Feedback feels heavy. Direction feels easy. Try: “What would ‘great’ look like here?” You’ll get real answers faster. 4. Make your work portable If your work can’t be explained in one sentence, it’s invisible. Practice: “This helps because ____.” 5. End everything with a next step People trust momentum. Always leave them with: “Next I’ll do X by Y.” — If you made it this far, you’re truly dedicated! ❤️ #earlycareer #corporatelife #careeradvice #professionaldevelopment

documents.source_expression: Infosys InStep is a highly prestigious, all-expenses-paid global internship program that allows students to work on live, high-impact projects. It has been consistently ranked as the No. 1 internship globally by Vault Firsthand. Eligibility & Application: Who Can Apply: Open to undergraduate, postgraduate, and PhD students from various disciplines including Engineering, Business, and Liberal Arts. Stipend: Interns receive a competitive monthly stipend, typically ranging from ₹25,000 to ₹50,000. Duration: Most internships last between 8 to 12 weeks, though some research-based roles can extend further. Location: While primarily hosted at the Bangalore HQ, projects may also be available in other major hubs like Pune, Hyderabad, and Chennai. Process: Register: Apply via the official InStep Application Portal. Screening: Your profile is evaluated based on academic record and project fit. Interviews: Includes two telephonic technical rounds followed by a managerial round to assess cultural fit. Conversion: Exceptional performance can lead to a Pre-Placement Offer (PPO) for full-time roles at Infosys globally. Pro-Tip: Because this is a rolling program, it is best to apply 3–6 months in advance to secure your preferred project slot. #infosys #paidinternship #abhii_crux #explore #jobs

documents.source_expression: Abundance signals status. Scarcity signals social climbing. These 5 networking habits might feel strategic in the moment, but they quietly lower your status over time. 1. Leading with “So, what do you do?” “What’s your role?” “What company are you with?” It feels efficient, but signals that you’re sorting for value. Instead, open with context: “What brought you here tonight?” “How do you know the host?” “What’s been interesting so far?” 2. Only networking with people who are “useful” to you. Strategic to you. Transparent to everyone else. Parallel connections build reputation. They expand your understanding of the room. And lead to referrals you never predicted. Every conversation does not need to convert. 3. Glancing at a business card and pocketing it instantly. Even if you think you’re being polite by maintaining eye contact, speed reads as dismissal. A card represents someone’s work. Receive it intentionally. Pause. Read it. Acknowledge it. 4. Little white lies like “I’ll be right back” to exit a conversation. Polite on the surface. Avoidant underneath. Instead: “It was great meeting you. I’m going to circulate a bit. I hope we connect again.” Clarity signals status. 5. Scanning the room while someone is speaking to you. You cannot “optimize” relationships. You have to let them develop. The person in front of you should matter more than the person you haven’t met. Constantly looking up the ladder is a scarcity mentality. Social abundance builds the room. #networking #etiquette #highvaluewoman #oldmoney #habitus

documents.source_expression: Most people spend hours perfecting their resume. But recruiters often look at it for just a few seconds. They’re not reading every line. They’re scanning quickly for proof of impact. And the fastest way they spot it? Your action words. Not: ❌ “Responsible for…” ❌ “Assisted with…” ❌ “Worked on…” Instead use words that show ownership and results: ✔ Led ✔ Implemented ✔ Delivered ✔ Increased ✔ Reduced ✔ Launched ✔ Managed ✔ Improved Here’s the difference: ❌ “Responsible for managing projects” ✔ “Led cross-functional projects that improved delivery time by 25%” ❌ “Helped with reporting” ✔ “Developed weekly reports that identified trends and improved decision making” One sounds passive. The other shows real impact. And that’s what gets attention. 💬 Comment RESUME and I’ll send you a list of 50 strong resume action verbs you can use right away. 📌 Save this before updating your resume. #resumetips #careeradvice #jobsearchtips #corporatelife

documents.source_expression: After 12+ years in recruiting, I can usually tell in 10 minutes who’s getting the offer. Top candidates consistently do these 5 things differently: 1️⃣ They come prepared for the person. They know the interviewer’s name. They’ve looked them up on LinkedIn. They understand how that role connects to the team. Opening with, “I saw you moved from engineering into product. I’d love to hear how that shaped this team.” That’s signal. 2️⃣ They treat it like a conversation. They engage. They build on answers. They don’t sit back and wait. Hiring managers remember how you made them feel. 3️⃣ They ask clarifying questions. “Are you looking for a leadership example or a cross-functional one?” “Is this about speed or long-term impact?” Five seconds of clarity can turn a good answer into a great one. 4️⃣ They use CARL, not vague storytelling. Context. Action. Result. Learning. That last piece separates strong candidates. Companies want learn-it-alls. 5️⃣ Their best questions come from the interview itself. Not something Googled. Something you heard. “You mentioned stakeholder alignment was tough last quarter. What shifted?” Now you’re memorable. And your thank-you note writes itself. Most people think interviews are about saying the right things. They’re about signaling the right behaviors. If you want to become the candidate hiring managers remember… DM me OFFER. And send this to someone interviewing this week. #jobsearch #interviewtips #careergrowth #midcareer #hiring

documents.source_expression: no internship yet? here’s 5 things that you can actually do… IT’S ONLY MARCH. YOU’RE NOT COOKED -most companies are done hiring but startups, local companies & nonprofits hire through april & may so look locally and keep applying while you build your backup plan. BUILD SOMETHING REAL -look at internship job descriptions you want -learn those exact skills and build a project using them that solves a real problem -help a local restaurant/ small biz with their website, social media, anything in your field -offer to do an unpaid internship (experience > nothing, always) DOCUMENT EVERYTHING -post your progress on linkedln -build a personal portfolio or a github profile as you go -update your resume / CV with everything you are doing -people can’t value what they can’t see so share what you’re doing. DON’T SLEEP ON NETWORKING -make a list of 10-20 target companies, look up people who work in your target department and reach out to them for a quick chat, not a job. -reach out to professors, many have summer research projects -connect with seniors who already have internships and ask for coffee chats / advice -shadow professionals in your field even just 2x a week AND YES SOFT SKILLS MATTER TOO -get a customer service job, volunteer, join a club -practice talking about your work out loud 4 months. 2-3 hours on skills building + 2 hours of networking daily. that’s all it takes to be 2x ahead by next summer💻 you’re not behind. take advantage of this summer and utilize the time well. {summer plans, student life, summer backup, skill building} #summerplans #explore

documents.source_expression: Comment “Resume” and I’ll send you all the prompts 👇 What happens when you ask Claude to rewrite your resume so it’s basically un-rejectable? I tried this and got 4 interviews in 24 hours. Prompt 1: Upload your resume and job description and say: “Act as a senior recruiter for this exact company. Analyze my resume against this job description and give me a match score out of 100, the top 5 missing keywords, and the 3 red flags a hiring manager would spot in under 10 seconds.” Prompt 2: “Rewrite my experience section to naturally include those keywords and remove the red flags. Use the Google XYZ formula: Accomplish X as measured by Y by doing Z.” Prompt 3: “Now act as an ATS filter and a hiring manager reading 200 resumes in one sitting. Scan my new resume and tell me which sections would get skipped, then rewrite them so they actually stop the scroll.” Bonus: Go to Claude Cowork and say: “Now that my resume is optimized, go to my LinkedIn and apply to 10 jobs I have the highest chance of landing an interview for. Customize the application for each one based on the job description.” Claude literally applies to jobs for you while you sleep. #claude #ai #resume #jobsearch #career

documents.source_expression: How I Connect Instantly with Anyone: Tip #1: Meet People Where They Are. the #1 mistake people make in an interview? they care more about their next question than the person in front of them. i prepped a month of questions for colman domingo. dream interview for me. he walked up last in line on the carpet, and he was exhausted. you could see it on him. so i threw the questions out. i didn’t open with anything i’d written. i opened with where he actually was. “looks like you had to muster up some energy for this one... what is this, your 128th premiere?” he laughed. the room got lighter. then i asked the one thing i was actually curious about in that moment. i’d noticed he plays hugo with his hands open, the way his partner raúl has sat for twelve years. “oh,” he said. “you noticed that.” that’s the whole thing. that’s connection. people think a killer interview is about the perfect question. it’s not. it’s two people actually meeting. the audience isn’t watching for the answer. they’re watching to feel two humans connect. i’ve spent 14 years interviewing some of the biggest names in the world, and this is the stuff no one teaches you. so i’m going to start giving it away. follow me as i break down how i connect instantly, one interview at a time. #interviewtips #redcarpet #hosting howtoconnect interviews

documents.source_expression: Baca ini ⬇️ Hallo teman-teman! Kamu mahasiswa semester 2–6? Dan lagi cari magang yang nggak cuma pengalaman, tapi juga dibayar? Nah, ini aku spill beberapa program magang paid yang bisa kamu target dari sekarang. 1. Smart I-Join Internship – Sinar Mas Program populer, terbuka untuk banyak jurusan, dan kamu bisa dapat pengalaman langsung di perusahaan besar. 2. Wings Internship Program – Wings Group Cocok buat kamu yang tertarik di dunia FMCG. Cukup kompetitif, tapi worth it buat pengalaman. 3. Altama Internship Program – Altama Surya Anugerah Pilihan menarik buat kamu yang ingin belajar langsung di industri manufaktur & retail. 4. Program Magang Bestee – BTPN Syariah Program yang cukup dikenal, dengan pengalaman + benefit lainnya yang bisa kamu dapatkan. ⚠️ Tapi ingat… Masuk ke program ini tetap butuh persiapan: • CV yang rapi • Skill yang relevan • Dan keberanian buat apply Tulis di komentar: “MAGANG” nanti kasih tips bikin proposal magang yang bikin kamu auto lolos! Dan jangan lupa Follow, karena aku bakal sering share tips seputar magang yang benar-bener bisa kamu praktekkan 🚀 #magangmahasiswa #internships #tipsmahasiswa #mahasiswamalang #mahasiswaugm

documents.source_expression: Strada's recent research on entry-level hiring in the AI era is reshaping how a lot of academic leaders are thinking about experiential strategy. The core finding is that employers are starting to view graduates with project-based learning experience as comparably valuable to candidates with internship experience. That equivalence is new, and it tells us something important about what employers are actually screening for. Employers are increasingly looking for the durable human capabilities that AI cannot easily replace: communication, critical thinking, working through ambiguity, and the ability to deliver real professional work alongside real stakeholders. Those are exactly the capabilities authentic project-based work develops, which is why employers are reading PBL experience as a credible signal of professional readiness. That changes what career-connected learning needs to deliver. The question employers ask is whether a graduate can do the work themselves, and project-based learning provides concrete evidence. More on this signal and others in our upcoming research brief. 👉 Read Strada's full analysis: See link in bio. #HigherEducation #CareerReadiness #ProjectBasedLearning #FutureOfWork #AIInEducation

documents.source_expression: ⁨ ⁨ ⁨ ⁨ ⁨ اومده بودم فقط دو ماه کارآموزی کنم ولی بعد فهمیدم هاب اصفهان جایی نیست که بذاره تو همون آدم روز اول بمونی ~ پس الان سرپرست واحد بین‌الملل شدم😭🌱 پس فعلاً در یک نقش جدید همچنان مشغول شیطنت کردن، جلسه‌ گذاشتن و دویدن بین پروژه‌های مختلف‌ام🌱✨ ممنون از آقای گودرزی که تو این مسیر همراه من بودن، چالش‌های جدیدی جلوی روم گذاشتن که بتونم ابعادی از وجودم کشف کنم که تا قبل از اون، از وجودشون خبر نداشتم و البته نقطه اوج ماجرا؟ دریافت گواهی پایان دوره‌ام از دستان دکتر عباسی ~ مدیرعامل صندوق سرمایه‌گذاری خطرپذیر فولاد مبارکه پی‌نوشت: دوران، دوران سخت و عجیبی بود! این کلیپ فقط لحظات شاد نشون داده چون همگی میدونیم چه گذشت و چی شد. بی‌اینترنتی برای کارآموزی که برای نشون دادن توانایی‌هاش و تداوم حضورش نیازمند به ابزارهایی مثل لینکدین و اینستاگرامه، بدترین اتفاق ممکن بود. هزینه‌های گزاف کانفیگ و فیلترشکن و دورکاری ناشی از جن/.گ به کنار.. My internship at HUB Isfahan has officially come to an end. 🎓 Apparently （though） nobody remembered to kick me out of the building🥸 Sooo.. here I am, continuing the journey in a new role—still causing trouble, scheduling meetings, sending emails, and running projects🌱✨ A big thank you to HUB Isfahan and Mr. Goodarzi for believing in me, challenging me, and helping me discover capabilities I didn’t even know I had. Somewhere between “just an intern” and countless meetings, projects, and international collaborations, I found a version of myself that was more confident, capable, and ambitious than ever. And as if that wasn’t enough, I had the honor of receiving my internship certificate from Dr. Abbasi himself. [ So yes, honored twice😎🫶🏼 ] Grateful for the trust, the opportunities, and the people who made this journey unforgettable⁩😭🫪 #internlife #internships #internship_diaries #internationalrelation

documents.source_expression: Recruiters will ask this. Be ready. “Why did you leave your job without another one lined up?” What NOT to say: ❌ “I was burned out” ❌ “My manager was toxic” ❌ “I just needed a break” What TO say instead: ✅ “I reached a point where my growth plateaued, and I wanted to be intentional about my next step.” ✅ “I left to focus on finding a role where I could have a bigger impact long-term.” ✅ “I used the time to sharpen my skills and be selective rather than rush into the wrong fit.” Key rule: Frame it as strategy, not escape. Hiring managers don’t punish gaps. They punish lack of clarity. 📌 Save this before your next interview.

documents.source_expression: Watch@Anna Papalia 3 Reasons Interns Get Fired If you’re about to start an internship position do not do these 5 things The only reason companies have Internship programs is so they can see who they want to hire in the fall. If they don’t like you. If you don’t make yourself useful then they won’t ask you back. If no one knows who you are you’re not getting a job offer. 1️⃣ Do not act like an entitled brat. I don’t care where you went to school or who your dad is, you don’t deserve the corner office. 2️⃣ Being known is important. ✅Learn how to introduce yourself. Google how to have small talk. Force yourself out of your comfort zone. Make yourself talk to 5 new people a day. 3️⃣Internships are full of boring, tedious unfun work. Ya gotta learn the basics and pay your dues before we give you cool projects. ✅Be enthusiastic and eager to learn. I know you think you know everything but you don’t. 4️⃣ This is not school. You need to find projects to work on. In school you finish an assignment and wait for your teacher to tell you what to do. Not in the corporate world. ✅If you finish a project, be proactive and find another one or just do something without being told. That shared drive isn’t organized, organize it. Your department needs help with an upcoming event, volunteer. We don’t want to have to hold your hand. 5️⃣ We want to hire you but if you are not enthusiastic and eager to learn we won’t ask you to come back. 🔥Bonus tip, every 50 year old is going to ask you for help with their computer. Be generous and help them. documents.source_expression: It took me five years to learn what nobody tells you on day one. The office runs on rules no one writes down. Here are 8 unspoken rules of office politics: 1️⃣ Your work doesn’t speak for itself. You have to. Great work hidden in a folder doesn’t count. Share progress, communicate results, and make sure the right people know your name is behind it. 2️⃣ Not every battle is worth fighting. Save your energy for decisions that matter. Winning small fights just burns political capital you’ll need later. 3️⃣ Likability is a career asset. You don’t have to be everyone’s friend, but people promote the people they actually enjoy working with. Competent and cold only gets you so far. 4️⃣ Your manager’s priorities are your priorities. Disagree privately. Align publicly. Fighting your manager in front of others doesn’t make you principled. It makes you look uncoachable. 5️⃣ Email is a paper trail, not a conversation. Write every message like someone else will read it, because they will. 6️⃣ Who you eat lunch with matters. Relationships aren’t built in meetings. They’re built in the in-between moments. Build your network before you need it. 7️⃣ Silence is a position. If you don’t speak up, people assume you agree or have nothing to add. Influence requires a voice. 8️⃣ Your reputation is built in rooms you’re not in. People talk. Make sure what they say about you helps your future, not your past. Office politics isn’t optional. Learn the rules or get left behind.

documents.source_expression: Stop applying into the void. Here's why it keeps happening & how to fix it. 👇 Most job sites weren't built for you. They were built for people with 5+ years of experience who already know someone on the inside. When a student or recent grad applies there, the resume goes in, and nothing comes out. That's not a you problem. That's a platform problem. WayUp is different. It's built specifically for college students and recent grads. Which means the employers on it are actually looking for people at your level. No "entry-level requires 3 years of experience." No black hole. Just real opportunities with companies that want to hire early-career talent. Here's what you get: → Exclusive jobs and internships you won't find anywhere else → Direct connections to employers who are actively recruiting students → Career tools to help you stand out before you even hit apply If you've been doing everything right and still hearing nothing back, the problem isn't your resume. documents.source_expression: When you apply on Naukri, LinkedIn Easy Apply, or Indeed, your profile goes straight into an ATS queue behind 400 other applicants. A recruiter who has never worked in your domain gives it 6 seconds. If the keywords do not match perfectly, it gets filtered out before a human even opens it. The hiring manager, the actual person who would decide whether you get hired, never gets your name. TrySideDoor skips that entirely. It finds the hiring manager directly at companies actively hiring for your exact role, and sends them a personalised cold email on your behalf. No job portal, no apply button, no ATS filter deciding your fate before anyone reads your resume. One client got a reply from the MD and CEO of a billion dollar company in 48 hours after 47 failed applications. Whether you are a fresher, career switcher, or experienced professional job hunting in Bangalore, Mumbai, Delhi NCR, Pune, Hyderabad, or open to remote roles, this is how people are actually getting hired in 2026. documents.source_expression: This is day 5 of my series on college mistakes that cost thousands Every semester, professors set aside office hours just to help students. But most students never show up. They struggle alone, lose easy marks on assignments, miss opportunities to improve their grades, and then wonder why they didn't get the internship or graduate school offer they wanted. A higher GPA can make you a stronger candidate for scholarships, internships, and your first job. Ignoring free academic support can have long-term consequences. Here's what top students do differently. They go to office hours before they're falling behind. They ask professors how to improve their assignments. They clarify confusing topics. And over time, they build relationships with professors who may later write recommendation letters or connect them with research and internship opportunities. One conversation can change your college career. This is day 5 of my series on college mistakes that cost thousands Follow for day 6. I make videos about boosting your GPA fast and building your career as a college student or graduate. #CollegeTips #CollegeStudents #CollegeLife #CareerAdvice #StudentSuccess

documents.source_expression: Your AI cover letter is the reason your resume never gets seen. 🚫 I posted ONE job on Naukri. 1,000 applications came in. My team opened maybe 100. The other 900? Deleted without a glance. Here's the brutal truth from 26 years in recruitment: The moment a recruiter spots an AI-generated cover letter — long, robotic, perfectly formatted with zero personality — they DON'T open the resume. Even if you're a perfect fit. Even if your CV is stellar. They don't have time. They have 900 other emails to ignore. 💡 The Fix: → Write 3-4 authentic lines → No ChatGPT fluff → Be real, be human, be specific → AI can be detected in seconds by experienced recruiters Your cover letter is not a formality. It's a GATEKEEPER. And AI is slamming the gate shut. #CoverLetter #JobSearch #ChatGPT #CareerAdvice #AIRejection #JobApplication #Naukri #Recruitment #Freshers #CareerCoach

documents.source_expression: Job Seekers: Stop Hiding Behind Safe Moves You are tired. You are frustrated. And you are wondering what to do next. But not every job search move deserves the same energy. Some moves feel productive. Others actually create opportunities. Here are 5 common moves job seekers make: ❌ Take any Scrum Master contract? 6/10 Better than a growing gap, but only if it gives you a real delivery story for future interviews. ❌ Get another certification? 2/10 One more badge will not fix weak positioning, no interview calls, or unclear proof. ❌ Lower your salary expectations? 3/10 Confidence attracts opportunities. Desperation usually weakens your negotiation. ✅ Cold-message Delivery Managers and Hiring Leaders? 9/10 Most candidates never do it. That is exactly why it works. ✅ Build real conversations instead of submitting more applications? 10/10 Referrals, relationships, and warm conversations open more doors than job boards. The biggest mistake is spending months polishing your resume while avoiding the uncomfortable actions that create movement. Stop asking: “What else should I fix?” Start asking: “What conversation am I avoiding?” Which move surprised you most? documents.source_expression: If your website greets visitors with a generic "How can I help you?" bot, you're not innovating — you're adding friction. A chatbot with no knowledge of your products, policies, or common customer issues can't actually solve anything. It just forces customers to repeat themselves, sift through irrelevant answers, or give up and look for a human. Here's the shift most businesses miss: the problem isn't AI. It's context. Customers don't want a chatbot for the sake of having one. They want a specific answer to the specific problem they showed up with. That only happens when AI is connected to a knowledge layer — a system built around your actual business: your products, your policies, your recurring customer questions, your voice. When AI can pull from that layer instead of guessing, everything changes: → Fewer frustrated customers, because context eliminates the back-and-forth loop → Faster resolutions, because answers are catered instead of scripted → Smarter use of AI, because you're solving real problems instead of checking a "we have a chatbot" box I built exactly this for my own business — a knowledge and context layer that lets AI understand what a customer is actually asking before it responds. The difference is night and day. Instead of AI that feels like a wall, it feels like a shortcut: faster answers, less friction, and resolution without ever needing a human in the loop. This is the missing piece for most businesses experimenting with AI right now. They're adding the technology without adding the understanding behind it — and customers can tell the difference immediately. If you're thinking about how to make AI actually useful on your site (not just present), start with the knowledge layer, not the chatbot interface. The interface is the easy part. The context is what makes it work. We're currently offering a 10-day free trial for teams who want to build this out — happy to share more if you're exploring this for your own business. AI isn't the problem. Context is. Fix that, and your AI finally starts doing what it was supposed to do all along: actually help your customers.

documents.source_expression: 1. Linkedin.com Obvious one but most people actually use this site completely wrong. Applying on LinkedIn is a waste of time. Instead just treat it like a resume and use the messaging and search features instead. Connect directly with hiring managers and follow up with them. This shows youre so much more dedicated and makes them remember you. 2. Intern-list.com What makes this my top pick for internship databases is the sheer variety of roles spanning countless career fields. The one caveat is that listings include both paid and unpaid positions, so it’s worth taking the time to filter carefully. Make sure to turn on notifications so you’re among the first to hear about new postings — getting your application in early can make a real difference!​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​. 3. Jobright.ai This all-in-one job tool is actually how I landed my first internship! It covers everything you need for a successful job search — from job matching to tailored resumes and plenty more. Once I took the time to optimize my recruiting profile, I started hearing back from companies much more frequently, which ultimately led to me securing an internship. If you’re looking to streamline the search process and actually land a role, this one is a must-try!​​​​​​​​​​​​​​. Follow me for more job hunting tips to secure your next internship! #student #jobless #career #jobsearch #fyp
